mnemonics: HAL: Hardware Description Language - Verilog Acts as a blueprint for Logic circuits
keyPoints: Verilog uses module keyword to define a hardware block with inputs and outputs
Continuous assignment (assign) models combinational logic where output updates automatically with input changes
The always @(*) block is used for procedural modeling of combinational logic, requiring careful use of blocking assignments (=)
commonMistakes: Using non-blocking assignments (<=) in combinational always blocks, which can cause simulation mismatches
Incomplete sensitivity lists in always blocks (e.g., missing an input), leading to incorrect behavior - solved by using always @(*)
Not initializing variables or registers, resulting in undefined states in simulation
